findings.location: s3 (自動インジェクション)
findings.description: The source contains a footnote note clarifying that loose coupling via DI container is a general DI container benefit—not specific to autowiring—and that this benefit is available even without using autowiring. This context is relevant for decisions about when to use autowiring vs explicit injection and is absent from s3.
findings.source_evidence: ※互いに疎結合な状態のまま結合できるという利点は、DIコンテナの持つ特性であり、自動インジェクションを使用しない場合でもDIコンテナを使用することでこのメリットは享受できる。

findings.description: `repository_import_override_priority` is referenced in the source for s2 alongside `repository_import_dir_override_priority` as the priority rule that governs same-name settings across separate config files, but it is absent from s2 hints.
findings.source_evidence: 別々の設定ファイルに記述された同名の設定は :ref:`repository_import_override_priority` および :ref:`repository_import_dir_override_priority` に記述した優先順位で評価される。

findings.description: s4 code examples explicitly reference the PascalCase class names `HelloComponent`, `HelloMessageProvider`, and `BasicHelloMessageProvider`, but none of these appear in s4 hints. Per V4 rules, PascalCase class names from section content should be included in hints.
findings.source_evidence: s4 XML: class="nablarch.core.repository.di.example.autowirebytype.HelloComponent" / class="nablarch.core.repository.di.example.autowirebytype.BasicHelloMessageProvider" / Java code: HelloComponent, HelloMessageProvider
